nodes.norm_label: pattern 2 — plan → build pipeline, chained one-shots (verified)

nodes.norm_label: pattern 3 — direct chat via agent api (verified)

nodes.norm_label: pattern 3: direct chat via agent api

nodes.norm_label: config.yaml has expanded api key instead of literal string

nodes.label: Connect from another container on the same network
nodes.norm_label: connect from another container on the same network

nodes.label: Installation A — Base Image Venv (Active Runtime)
nodes.norm_label: installation a — base image venv (active runtime)

nodes.label: Gap Report: Dual Hermes Installation Investigation
nodes.norm_label: gap report: dual hermes installation investigation

nodes.label: 1. Inventory of All Hermes Installation Points
nodes.norm_label: 1. inventory of all hermes installation points

nodes.label: Installation A -- Base Image Venv (ACTIVE at runtime)
nodes.norm_label: installation a -- base image venv (active at runtime)

nodes.label: Path 3: Staged Agent → Deps Pipeline (Installation B)
nodes.norm_label: path 3: staged agent → deps pipeline (installation b)

nodes.label: Option C: Eliminate Staged Agent (NOT RECOMMENDED)
nodes.norm_label: option c: eliminate staged agent (not recommended)

nodes.label: Hermes WebUI Service (:8787)
nodes.rationale: Browser-based chat UI hosted on port 8787. Hosts AIAgent in-process via Python ThreadingHTTPServer. Bind address must be 0.0.0.0 inside container for host port mapping to work.

nodes.label: Hermes Gateway Service (:8642)
nodes.rationale: OpenAI-compatible aiohttp API server on port 8642. Runs hermes-agent server-side with tools, memory, skills. Refuses 0.0.0.0 binding without API_SERVER_KEY set.

nodes.label: OpenCode Serve Service (:4096)
nodes.rationale: Headless OpenCode HTTP server on port 4096 for remote attach. Opt-in via OPENCODE_SERVE_ENABLED (default false) because serve exits immediately without LLM provider and would tear container down via wait -n.

nodes.label: Model Discovery Mechanism
nodes.rationale: Auto-discovers chat models from OPENAI_BASE_URL/models at startup. Filters non-chat (embed, whisper, tts, dall-e, etc.) and wildcard patterns (/*). Falls back to OPENAI_DEFAULT_MODEL on failure.

nodes.label: Wildcard Model Filter (IDs ending in /*)
nodes.rationale: Filters litellm model-group patterns (anthropic/*, openai/*, deepseek/*, openrouter/*, vertex_ai/*) that resolve to 404 errors when used as actual model IDs.

nodes.label: Non-Chat Model Filter
nodes.rationale: Filters models matching: embed, whisper, tts, dall-e, sora, image, realtime, transcrib, moderat, audio, codegen, babbage, davinci, curie, ada, text-, stable, midjourney, flux, /sd/, mj, replicate, resolution.

nodes.label: Config Generation (config.yaml + opencode.jsonc)
nodes.rationale: Entrypoint regenerates config.yaml and opencode.jsonc from env vars on every boot. Both must write model.default AND model.name to satisfy WebUI's models_cache.json builder and hermes-agent's model resolution.

nodes.label: Two-Phase Skill Installation
nodes.rationale: Build-time install (60s, full git clones) plus runtime cp -a from staging (<1s). Eliminates network fetches at startup. Hermes skills staged at /opt/hermes-skills-staging; OpenCode skills persist in image layer.

nodes.label: OpenCode Security Modes (strict/standard/yolo)
nodes.rationale: strict=31 bash rules, interpreters denied, .env denied (default, production). standard=22 rules, interpreters allowed (development). yolo=allow all (trusted sandbox). Last-match-wins semantics.

nodes.label: cc-safety-net Plugin (PreToolUse Hook)
nodes.rationale: Blocks destructive git (reset --hard, push --force, clean -fd) and filesystem (rm -rf /, chmod 777) commands BEFORE OpenCode permission system. Active in all modes including yolo.

nodes.label: Cloudflare UA Patch (CustomProfile sed)
nodes.rationale: Build-time sed patch setting default_headers={User-Agent: hermes-agent/1.0} on CustomProfile class. Required because Cloudflare WAF blocks default OpenAI/Python SDK User-Agent with HTTP 403.

nodes.label: Multi-Step Docker Build Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Dockerfile builds single image with WebUI base, system packages, Node.js 22, OpenCode CLI, staged agent source, Cloudflare patch, and pre-installed skills. Targets Linux ARM64. Agent cloned to staging path, not runtime bind mount.

nodes.label: Entrypoint Sequence (13-step startup)
nodes.rationale: Sequential startup with health gates: skills staging copy → model discovery → config gen → agent copy → WebUI (:8787) → wait → gateway (:8642) → wait → opencode serve (:4096, opt-in) → wait -n.

nodes.label: Volume Layout (bind mounts)
nodes.rationale: volumes_hermes_opencode/ separates build context from runtime data. Bind mounts: ./data/hermes-home → /home/hermeswebui/.hermes (agent config, sessions, skills); ./data/workspace → /workspace.

nodes.label: OpenCode Plugin System (npm-resolved)
nodes.rationale: Three plugins: @tarquinen/opencode-dcp (context pruning), @franlol/opencode-md-table-formatter, cc-safety-net. Resolved via Node.js npm at $HOME/.config/opencode/node_modules/. Requires Node.js 22.

nodes.label: Acceptance Criteria (AC1-AC29)
nodes.rationale: 29 acceptance criteria in PRD covering build success, healthcheck, WebUI, agent presence, UA patch, config generation, OpenCode availability, LLM calls, secrets hygiene, second boot speed, model discovery, security mode, skills, graphify.

nodes.label: Bats E2E Test Suite
nodes.rationale: 59 bats tests under tests/e2e/ run via tests/run.sh. Builds image, starts stack, waits for healthy, runs bats. Maps to AC1-AC29. Triggered on push/PR to main via GitHub Actions.

nodes.label: Graphify Integration (cross-platform skill)
nodes.rationale: graphifyy PyPI package installed via uv tool install. Registers skills for both OpenCode and Hermes at build time using HOME=/home/hermeswebui override. Binary at /usr/local/bin/graphify. Runtime re-registration is overlayfs safety net.

nodes.label: Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent)
nodes.rationale: AI agent runtime. Git cloned at build to /opt/hermes-agent-staging. Copied to bind mount /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/hermes-agent on first boot. Version pinned via HERMES_AGENT_VERSION build arg.

nodes.label: HERMES_API_KEY (auto-generated gateway token)
nodes.rationale: Bearer token for Agent API :8642. Auto-generated as hermes-<32 hex chars> when empty, printed to container logs. Required for 0.0.0.0 binding because api_server refuses without key.

nodes.label: Build-Time Staging Paths (/opt/*-staging)
nodes.rationale: Agent at /opt/hermes-agent-staging (~50MB), skills at /opt/hermes-skills-staging (~10MB). Separation required because runtime paths are bind mounts that start empty on first boot. Staging paths persist in image layer.

nodes.label: /workspace Bind Mount
nodes.rationale: User project workspace mounted at /workspace inside container, configurable via HERMES_WORKSPACE env. Always trusted by resolve_trusted_workspace() because DEFAULT_WORKSPACE=/workspace is hardcoded.

nodes.label: hermeswebui User Isolation (UID 1000)
nodes.rationale: Gateway and opencode serve run as hermeswebui via su -s /bin/bash. Reduces filesystem blast radius — cannot write to /usr/local/bin, /opt, /root. Entrypoint (PID 1) runs as root.

nodes.label: @tarquinen/opencode-dcp (Context Pruner)
nodes.rationale: Context transform plugin that prunes low-relevance messages from conversation to reduce token usage. npm spec @tarquinen/opencode-dcp@latest.

nodes.label: @franlol/opencode-md-table-formatter
nodes.rationale: Output transform plugin that normalizes markdown tables in tool output for consistent rendering. npm spec @franlol/opencode-md-table-formatter@latest.

nodes.label: state.db (SQLite Session Store)
nodes.rationale: SQLite database at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/state.db stores session history. Persisted across container restarts via bind mount. Should be backed up when container is stopped to avoid corruption.

nodes.label: X-Hermes-Session-Id Header (Session Continuity)
nodes.rationale: HTTP header to continue existing conversation via gateway. Pair with X-Hermes-Session-Key for long-term memory scoping.

nodes.label: wait -n Process Supervisor
nodes.rationale: Bash builtin used by entrypoint to block until any background process (WebUI, gateway, opencode serve) exits. Propagates any crash as container exit. This is why OPENCODE_SERVE_ENABLED defaults to false — opencode serve exits without LLM provider and would tear container down.

nodes.label: Node.js 22 Requirement
nodes.rationale: Installed via nodesource setup_22.x. Required for OpenCode's npm-based plugin resolution at runtime. Constraint C9 in PRD.

nodes.label: Docker Healthcheck (bash healthcheck.sh)
nodes.rationale: Healthcheck runs bash /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh every 10s with 120s start_period and 10 retries. Used by tests/run.sh wait loop and GitHub Actions to gate test execution.

nodes.label: OpenAI-Compatible API Surface
nodes.rationale: Gateway exposes /v1/chat/completions, /v1/models, /v1/responses, /v1/runs. Compatible with Open WebUI, LobeChat, LibreChat, AnythingLLM, ChatBox, NextChat.

nodes.label: portainer-cloudflare-traefik_default Network
nodes.rationale: External docker network defined in docker-compose.override.yml. Service joins with alias vanilla-hermes-opencode. Used by Portainer/Cloudflare/Traefik reverse proxy stack.

nodes.label: Seven Skill Upstream Sources
nodes.rationale: anthropics/skills (6 OpenCode), openai/skills (4 OpenCode), multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills (1 OpenCode), coding-agents-docs-guideline (1 OpenCode), opencode-plan-build-orchestrator (1 both), phuryn/pm-skills (~55 Hermes), graphifyy PyPI (1 both). Total 14 OpenCode + ~67 Hermes skills.

nodes.label: model.default AND model.name Keys
nodes.rationale: Both keys must be written to config.yaml. WebUI's get_effective_default_model() reads model.default without fallback. hermes-agent reads model.default with fallback to model.name. Missing default → empty models_cache.json. Constraint C7 in PRD.

nodes.label: Linux ARM64 Target Platform
nodes.rationale: Must build and run on ARM64 (Raspberry Pi CM5). Constraint C1. ARM64 overlayfs may drop new layers — motivates runtime graphify re-registration and inline command: docker-compose overrides.

nodes.label: Secrets Handling (key_env vs literal)
nodes.rationale: No hardcoded secrets in tracked files (public repo). config.yaml uses key_env: OPENAI_API_KEY (literal string, not expanded value). opencode.jsonc uses {env:OPENAI_API_KEY} interpolation. Constraint C2, C3.

nodes.label: E2E Test Workflow (GitHub Actions)
nodes.rationale: Runs bats e2e tests on every push/PR to main, with secretless CI fallback via mock LLM server.

nodes.label: Mock LLM Server for CI
nodes.rationale: Enables full CI test suite without real LLM provider secrets. Conditionally started when no OPENAI_API_KEY secret exists. Fallback values substituted for all secrets so container starts against the mock and all model discovery/config generation proceeds normally.

nodes.label: .env Seeding from GitHub Secrets
nodes.rationale: Creates .env at runtime from GitHub secrets with mock-key fallbacks so the container boots identically with or without real credentials.

nodes.label: Collect Logs on Failure
nodes.rationale: Captures docker compose logs and container inspect on test failure for debugging CI regressions.

nodes.label: Container: hermes-opencode
nodes.rationale: Single container running WebUI + Gateway + OpenCode in one process tree to simplify deployment on ARM64 Raspberry Pi.

nodes.label: Mandated Skills Policy
nodes.rationale: Five skills (karpathy-guidelines, security-best-practices, webapp-testing, coding-agents-docs-guideline, yeet) must be loaded on every task for consistent quality.

nodes.label: No shell=True Rule
nodes.rationale: Prevents shell injection vulnerabilities in subprocess calls.

nodes.label: No Hardcoded Secrets Rule
nodes.rationale: Repo is public; secrets must use env vars or key_env references only.

nodes.label: No Wildcard Model Patterns Rule
nodes.rationale: Wildcard patterns like /* cause 404 errors when used as model IDs; must be filtered during discovery.

nodes.label: Both model.default and model.name Rule
nodes.rationale: WebUI reads model.default; hermes-agent reads model.name as fallback. Both must be written to config.yaml.

nodes.label: CustomProfile User-Agent Header Rule
nodes.rationale: Cloudflare blocks OpenAI SDK default User-Agent. The hermes-agent/1.0 patch is critical for LLM calls to work through Cloudflare-fronted proxies.

nodes.label: Agent Source Goes to Staging Path
nodes.rationale: Agent source goes to /opt/hermes-agent-staging, not the runtime path, because the base image venv is the single active runtime.
nodes.norm_label: agent source goes to staging path

nodes.label: Linux ARM64 Target Platform
nodes.rationale: Target is Raspberry Pi; everything must build and run unattended on ARM64.

nodes.label: host.docker.internal extra_hosts Fix
nodes.rationale: Maps host.docker.internal to host-gateway inside container, fixing DNS resolution on bare Linux hosts (issues #27, #31).

nodes.label: Per-Model Provider Routing (issue #46)
nodes.rationale: _resolve_provider_prefix() in config-opencode.sh determines opencode/ vs litellm/ prefix independently per model based on raw model name and available credentials. Supports hybrid deployments where model routes through LiteLLM and small_model routes through Zen.

nodes.label: OpenCode Provider Block (issue #46)
nodes.rationale: When OPENCODE_API_KEY is set, an explicit opencode provider entry with apiKey:{env:OPENCODE_API_KEY} is generated. Also seeds auth.json as fallback credential store. Required for built-in opencode/ models to authenticate.

nodes.label: Security Modes (strict/standard/yolo)
nodes.rationale: Three permission profiles control OpenCode agent's blast radius. strict=31 bash rules (production), standard=22 (dev), yolo=0 (trusted sandbox).

nodes.label: Dual Installation Architecture
nodes.rationale: Installation is dual but NOT parallel. Base image venv (/app/venv/) is active runtime for WebUI+gateway. Staged clone (/opt/hermes-agent-staging/) is a deps pipeline feeding uv pip install into the venv.

nodes.label: Bash Heredoc JSON Break Rule
nodes.rationale: Bash heredoc JSON breaks with 300+ dynamic entries; use python3 -c json.dump() for config generation.

nodes.label: Docker overlayfs ARM64 Issue
nodes.rationale: Docker overlayfs on ARM64 may drop new layers. Modifications to existing files survive, new files may vanish. Use inline command: in docker-compose as workaround.

nodes.label: Graphify Skill
nodes.rationale: Installed as Hermes skill at build time. Builds knowledge graph from repo contents. Tested in test/08-graphify.bats.

nodes.label: Browser Human-in-the-Loop Capability
nodes.rationale: Chromium CDP for agent-browser cooperation when BROWSER_HUMAN_LOOP_ENABLED=true.

nodes.label: LLM-Wiki Capability
nodes.rationale: Personal knowledge base at ~/.hermes/wiki with [[wikilinks]]. Persists on bind mount.

nodes.label: Hermes x OpenCode Docker Stack (Product)
nodes.rationale: A Docker Compose stack running Hermes WebUI + Hermes Agent + OpenCode CLI in a single container with three exposed services.

nodes.label: Hermes WebUI (:8787)
nodes.rationale: Browser-based chat interface. Python server imports hermes_cli, creates AIAgent in-process.

nodes.label: Hermes Agent API (:8642)
nodes.rationale: OpenAI-compatible endpoint /v1/chat/completions for external client integration.

nodes.label: OpenCode Serve (:4096)
nodes.rationale: Headless server for remote opencode attach. Optional, gated by OPENCODE_SERVE_ENABLED.

nodes.label: Installation A — Active Runtime (/app/venv/)
nodes.rationale: The only code that executes. Pip-installed by /hermeswebui_init.bash at boot. Used by WebUI (AIAgent in-process) + Gateway (CLI binary).

nodes.label: Installation B — Staged Source (/opt/hermes-agent-staging/)
nodes.rationale: Passive staging clone, never executed. Required because /hermeswebui_init.bash installs agent deps from local source tree via uv pip install, not from PyPI. Also provides skills source for install-skills.sh.

nodes.label: CustomProfile User-Agent Patch
nodes.rationale: sed in Dockerfile adds User-Agent: hermes-agent/1.0 to custom model provider. Cloudflare blocks default OpenAI SDK User-Agent.

nodes.label: Image Bloat Mitigation (clone trimming)
nodes.rationale: Git clone includes ~200MB upstream skills/docs/tests not needed for deps. Dockerfile trims these after skill extraction.

nodes.label: Data Flow: User → WebUI/API → Agent → OpenCode → LLM
nodes.rationale: User sends chat via browser/API → Hermes Agent processes → OpenCode CLI (terminal tool) → LLM Provider (external).
nodes.norm_label: data flow: user → webui/api → agent → opencode → llm

nodes.label: Quick Start Guide
nodes.rationale: Clone, configure .env, docker compose up -d --build. First boot ~80-160s, subsequent ~25-50s.

nodes.label: Cloudflare 403 Troubleshooting (UA Fix)
nodes.rationale: OpenAI Python SDK default User-Agent gets blocked by Cloudflare-fronted providers. Stack patches CustomProfile at build time to send hermes-agent/1.0.

nodes.label: OpenCode Serve + Attach Limitation
nodes.rationale: opencode serve + attach pattern is experimental and not fully functional (issue #10). Disabled by default.

nodes.label: Automatic Model Discovery
nodes.rationale: When OPENAI_BASE_URL and OPENAI_API_KEY are set, entrypoint auto-discovers all chat models. Non-chat models and wildcards filtered out.

nodes.label: One-Shot Coding Pattern
nodes.rationale: opencode <dir> --prompt pattern avoids broken opencode run --agent plan/build commands.

nodes.label: CI Port Publishing Override
nodes.rationale: Publishes host ports for E2E test access. Base compose uses expose-only (no host ports). Tests curl localhost:8787/8642 so CI needs published ports.

nodes.label: Chrome CDP Port Publishing (:9222)
nodes.rationale: Publishes Chromium CDP port for browser human-in-the-loop testing in CI.

nodes.label: hermes-opencode Docker Compose Service
nodes.rationale: Single service definition with bind mounts, environment, healthcheck. Build context points to volumes_hermes_opencode/build/Dockerfile.

nodes.label: extra_hosts: host.docker.internal:host-gateway
nodes.rationale: Fix #31: host.docker.internal does not resolve on vanilla Linux Docker. Maps to host's gateway IP inside container. Works on all platforms.

nodes.label: Docker Healthcheck (healthcheck.sh)
nodes.rationale: Uses bash /usr/local/bin/healthcheck.sh instead of simple curl. start_period 120s for first boot dependency installation.

nodes.label: Expose-Only Ports (Commented Out)
nodes.rationale: Base compose uses expose-only by default (host ports commented out). Production uses reverse proxy; CI uses override file for port publishing.

nodes.label: Optional Wiki Volume Mount
nodes.rationale: HERMES_WIKI_VOLUME allows mounting host wiki directory for llm-wiki skill persistence and sharing personal wiki with agent.

nodes.label: WebUI ThreadingHTTPServer
nodes.rationale: Browser-based chat interface backed by Python ThreadingHTTPServer. Hosts Hermes Agent runtime in-process via from run_agent import AIAgent.

nodes.label: Two-Step Async Chat Flow
nodes.rationale: POST /api/chat/start returns stream_id immediately, spawns daemon thread running AIAgent.run_conversation(). GET /api/chat/stream is long-lived SSE connection. Avoids blocking on long LLM calls.

nodes.label: SSE Token Streaming
nodes.rationale: GET /api/chat/stream forwards tokens, tool calls, and completion events via Server-Sent Events.

nodes.label: Empty default_model in models_cache.json
nodes.rationale: WebUI generates models_cache.json with default_model sourced from config.yaml's model.default key. If model.default absent (only model.name set), default_model is empty and no model appears in UI. Entrypoint resolves by writing both keys.

nodes.label: Session Storage (SQLite state.db)
nodes.rationale: Sessions stored in SQLite at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/state.db. Persisted via bind mount across restarts.

nodes.label: Optional HMAC Cookie Auth
nodes.rationale: HERMES_WEBUI_PASSWORD enables HMAC-signed HTTP-only cookies with 24h TTL. No auth by default — acceptable behind firewall but must be addressed for network-exposed deployments.

nodes.label: Hermes Gateway (api_server platform)
nodes.rationale: aiohttp-based OpenAI-compatible API server on :8642. Exposes /v1/chat/completions, /v1/models, /v1/responses, /v1/runs.

nodes.label: Gateway Restart-Loop Supervisor
nodes.rationale: Gateway wrapped in while true restart loop. If it exits, auto-restarts with 2s delay. Logs to gateway-restart.log. Ensures gateway recovers from crashes without full container restart.

nodes.label: HERMES_HOME Resolution
nodes.rationale: Entrypoint runs as root (HOME=/root) but config.yaml is at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes. HERMES_HOME must be explicitly exported. Set in entrypoint.sh AND docker-compose.yml environment block.

nodes.label: API Key Auto-Generation
nodes.rationale: When HERMES_API_KEY is empty, entrypoint generates random key (hermes-<32hex>) and writes to config.yaml. Gateway refuses 0.0.0.0 bind without API_SERVER_KEY set.

nodes.label: Session Continuity (X-Hermes-Session-Id)
nodes.rationale: Pass X-Hermes-Session-Id header to continue existing conversation. X-Hermes-Session-Key for long-term memory scoping.

nodes.label: OpenCode Serve (Headless HTTP Server)
nodes.rationale: Exposes OpenCode CLI as remote-attachable endpoint on :4096. Runs server component without TUI client. Keeps MCP server connections warm.

nodes.label: OPENCODE_SERVE_ENABLED Gating
nodes.rationale: Serve is opt-in (default: false). Without LLM provider, serve exits immediately and tears container down via wait -n. Gating prevents this.

nodes.label: OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD Auth
nodes.rationale: Auto-generated random password written to /tmp/opencode-server-password and container logs. Clients pass via -p flag. Prevents unauthorized remote access.

nodes.label: opencode v1.16 --attach Session Regression
nodes.rationale: As of opencode v1.16, opencode run --attach returns exit 1 'Session not found' unless session exists. Previously auto-created. One-shot run --attach invocations fail without interactive attach first. Tests accept exit 1 as known regression.

nodes.label: opencode run with litellm/ prefix broken
nodes.rationale: OpenCode v1.16.2 hardcoded provider registry for opencode run CLI. Custom providers from config only load in TUI/serve mode. litellm/ prefix throws ProviderModelNotFoundError in CLI mode. Workaround: use opencode/ built-in models or serve+attach.
nodes.norm_label: opencode run with litellm/ prefix broken

nodes.label: Dockerfile (Multi-Step Build)
nodes.rationale: Builds single image with WebUI base + system packages + Node.js + OpenCode CLI + staged agent source + UA patch + skills.

nodes.label: HERMES_AGENT_VERSION Build Arg
nodes.rationale: Configurable agent version (branch/tag/commit). Defaults to main. Enables version pinning via --build-arg.

nodes.label: Agent Staging Separation
nodes.rationale: Agent cloned to /opt/hermes-agent-staging, not runtime path. Runtime path is bind-mounted volume starting empty on first boot. Entrypoint copies from staging on first start. Separation ensures agent baked into image, bind mount persists sessions/config/skills across rebuilds.

nodes.label: Skill Staging (Two-Phase)
nodes.rationale: Hermes skills staged to /opt/hermes-skills-staging because ~/.hermes is bind-mounted and overwrites at runtime. Entrypoint copies staging→bind mount at every boot (cp -a, near-instant). OpenCode skills have no volume mount, persist in image directly.

nodes.label: Graphify Build-Time Registration
nodes.rationale: Graphify registers for both platforms at build time. OpenCode skill written directly; Hermes skill written to HOME then copied to staging. Binary copied to /usr/local/bin for all-user access. Runtime re-run as overlayfs safety net.

nodes.label: Build-Time Verification Step
nodes.rationale: Final RUN step checks Python version, opencode version, agent present, patch applied. Catches missing agent, failed sed, or missing OpenCode before image is usable.

nodes.label: entrypoint.sh (Thin Orchestrator)
nodes.rationale: 78-81 line orchestrator that sources 12 library modules from scripts/lib/. All function logic lives in lib/ modules. Only calls functions and manages execution sequence.

nodes.label: Library Modules (scripts/lib/)
nodes.rationale: 12 sourced modules: constants.sh, runtime-env.sh, port-utils.sh, agent-setup.sh, model-discovery.sh, config-hermes.sh, config-opencode.sh, validate-opencode.sh, service-gateway.sh, service-opencode.sh, wiki-init.sh, service-browser-vnc.sh. Sourced in dependency order.

nodes.label: discover_models() Function
nodes.rationale: Curls OPENAI_BASE_URL/models with 15s timeout. Python3 inline filters non-chat models and wildcards. Falls back to OPENAI_DEFAULT_MODEL on failure. Sets DISCOVERED_MODELS.

nodes.label: generate_config() Function
nodes.rationale: Writes config.yaml with litellm custom provider, multi-model models dict, api_server platform. Auto-generates API key if empty. Writes both model.default and model.name.

nodes.label: generate_opencode_config() Function
nodes.rationale: Writes opencode.jsonc with plugins, permission block, provider config. Uses _resolve_provider_prefix() for per-model routing. Seeds auth.json as fallback. Copies config to root, symlinks session DB.

nodes.label: _resolve_provider_prefix() Function
nodes.rationale: Determines opencode/ vs litellm/ prefix independently per model. Decision table: opencode/* → opencode, litellm/* → litellm, any other + OPENAI_BASE_URL set → litellm, any other + no OPENAI_BASE_URL → opencode.

nodes.label: auth.json Fallback Credential Store
nodes.rationale: Seeds ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json with OPENCODE_API_KEY. Covers code paths that read credentials from auth store rather than config provider block. OPENCODE_API_KEY also passed explicitly through su in service-opencode.sh.

nodes.label: Root Config Copy (fix #28)
nodes.rationale: Copies opencode.jsonc to /root/.config/opencode/ so root sees providers when invoked via terminal() tool. Idempotent via readlink -f guard.

nodes.label: Session DB Symlink (fix #29)
nodes.rationale: Symlinks /root/.local/share/opencode → hermeswebui's data dir. Ensures root shares session DB. Idempotent.

nodes.label: validate_opencode_zen_key() (fix #30)
nodes.rationale: Validates OPENCODE_API_KEY against https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models with 10s timeout. Non-fatal — container always continues. Catches invalid keys early instead of discovering 401 at first use.

nodes.label: ensure_agent() Function
nodes.rationale: Copies agent from /opt/hermes-agent-staging to bind mount if not present. Idempotent — skips if pyproject.toml exists. Staged clone is deps source for /hermeswebui_init.bash, not second runtime.

nodes.label: append_skills_external_dirs()
nodes.rationale: Appends skills.external_dirs pointing to hermes-agent/optional-skills in config.yaml. Must run after ensure_agent copies dir into place. Makes 94 built-in + 72 custom = 166 total skills discoverable.

nodes.label: Sequential Startup with Health Gates
nodes.rationale: Services start in dependency order: WebUI → health check → gateway → health check → opencode serve. Each gate ensures dependencies ready before launch. wait -n keeps container alive.

nodes.label: Env-Driven Configuration Pattern
nodes.rationale: All runtime config via environment variables in .env. Eliminates config drift between environments. Same .env produces same configs every time. Keeps secrets out of tracked files.

nodes.label: Per-Model Provider Routing Decision Table
nodes.rationale: Backward compatibility: only behavioral change is in dual-provider mode. Previously both model and small_model got litellm prefix, causing 401 when small model was Zen-only. With per-model routing, each model gets correct prefix independently.

nodes.label: Explicit opencode Provider Block
nodes.rationale: When OPENCODE_API_KEY set, explicit opencode provider entry with apiKey:{env:OPENCODE_API_KEY} generated alongside litellm provider. Ensures built-in opencode/ models have explicit API key mapping rather than implicit resolution.

nodes.label: auth.json Credential Store Seeding
nodes.rationale: As fallback credential store, auth.json seeded with OPENCODE_API_KEY at ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json. Covers code paths reading from auth store rather than config provider block.

nodes.label: Runtime Environment Detection
nodes.rationale: detect_runtime_env() determines Docker/local/K8s. normalize_base_url_for_local() replaces host.docker.internal with localhost when running locally. Allows same .env to work in Docker and bare metal.

nodes.label: Zen API Key Validation Detail
nodes.rationale: Outbound HTTP to https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models. 10s timeout. Non-fatal. Catches invalid keys early.

nodes.label: OpenCode {env:VAR} Key Interpolation
nodes.rationale: OpenCode uses {env:VAR_NAME} interpolation for apiKey. Custom providers (@ai-sdk/openai-compatible) do not auto-detect env vars. Avoids hardcoded secrets.

nodes.label: Cosmetic has_key:false Issue
nodes.rationale: WebUI /api/providers reports has_key:false for custom:litellm because _provider_has_key() only checks literal api_key field, not key_env resolution. Chat works correctly regardless. Upstream display-only issue.

nodes.label: Bats E2E Test Suite (~109 tests)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic checklist for validating full stack after changes. Maps to 30 acceptance criteria. Catches regressions: missing agent, failed patch, config misgeneration, gateway failures, wildcard leakage.

nodes.label: 30 Acceptance Criteria (AC1-AC30)
nodes.rationale: Maps to PRD acceptance criteria. Covers build, health, agent source, UA patch, key_env, OpenCode, LLM calls, model discovery, gateway, skills, security, graphify, agent installation architecture.

nodes.label: Mock LLM Server Detail (tests/mock-llm-server.sh)
nodes.rationale: Single-file bash script embedding Python http.server.HTTPServer using only stdlib. Listens on 0.0.0.0:4000. CI conditionally starts it when no real OPENAI_API_KEY secret. Enables secretless CI.

nodes.label: Graphify Acceptance Criteria (AC26-AC29)
nodes.rationale: AC26: uv available, AC27: graphify CLI, AC28: Hermes graphify skill, AC29: OpenCode graphify skill. Verifies graphify integration at build and runtime.

nodes.label: Wiki Init Tests (WI1-WI5)
nodes.rationale: Wiki dir exists, SCHEMA.md exists, subdirs created, SCHEMA.md content, index/log exist.

nodes.label: Agent Installation Architecture Tests
nodes.rationale: Staged clone has pyproject.toml, runtime copy exists, agent not running from staging, UA patch applied, staged clone trimmed.

nodes.label: Non-Chat Model Filter
nodes.rationale: Removes models matching regex patterns: embed, whisper, tts, dall-e, sora, image, realtime, transcrib, moderat, audio, codegen, babbage, davinci, curie, ada, text-, stable, midjourney, flux, /sd/, mj, replicate, resolution, cli-proxy-api. Prevents errors if selected.

nodes.label: Wildcard/Model-Group Filter
nodes.rationale: Removes litellm model-group patterns ending with /* (anthropic/*, openai/*, deepseek/*, openrouter/*, vertex_ai/*). These are routing aliases returning 404 as actual model IDs.

nodes.label: Case-Insensitive Model Dedup
nodes.rationale: Model IDs deduplicated case-insensitively using model_id.lower() as set key. Prevents duplicates when provider returns same model with different casing. Original casing preserved in final list.

nodes.label: Model Discovery Fallback Behavior
nodes.rationale: On any failure (no BASE_URL, no API key, curl timeout, all filtered), DISCOVERED_MODELS = OPENAI_DEFAULT_MODEL only. Default model prepended if not in discovered list. Ensures container always starts.

nodes.label: Model Token Limits (get_limits())
nodes.rationale: Assigns context/output token limits per model via pattern matching against known families (gpt-4.1, gpt-4o, claude, deepseek, glm, gemini, etc.). Written into opencode.jsonc models dict. Used by OpenCode token budget calculation.

nodes.label: Dual Config Model List Sync
nodes.rationale: Single discovery pass feeds both config.yaml (Hermes) and opencode.jsonc (OpenCode). Ensures both services see identical model list. No manual model ID management needed.

nodes.label: install-skills.sh Script
nodes.rationale: Installs curated skills from 8 upstream sources into both OpenCode and Hermes skill directories. Runs at Docker build time with fast runtime staging copy for Hermes.

nodes.label: Two-Phase Skill Installation
nodes.rationale: Build phase: full install-skills.sh execution (~60s, git clones, pip installs). Runtime phase: cp -a staging → bind mount + graphify re-registration (<1s). Eliminates runtime network dependency.

nodes.label: Sparse Clone Helper (clone_sparse)
nodes.rationale: Uses git partial clone (--filter=blob:none --sparse) to avoid downloading entire repos. Only required directories fetched. Reduces build time and image size.

nodes.label: 8 Upstream Skill Sources
nodes.rationale: Anthropic skills, OpenAI skills, Karpathy guidelines, coding-agents-docs-guideline, opencode-plan-build-orchestrator, pm-skills, llm-wiki (from staging), graphify (PyPI).

nodes.label: Graphify HOME Override for Build
nodes.rationale: graphify install writes to $HOME-relative paths. During build HOME=/root. Must set HOME=/home/hermeswebui for skills to land in correct user home. Binary also copied to /usr/local/bin for all-user access.

nodes.label: Runtime graphify Re-run (overlayfs safety net)
nodes.rationale: Runtime entrypoint re-runs graphify install --platform hermes. Protects against ARM64 overlayfs issues where build-time new files may vanish. Recreates Hermes skill file if missing.

nodes.label: Delegation Pattern Matrix
nodes.rationale: Catalogues all agent-to-agent delegation patterns with status and reliability. Prevents wasted effort on broken patterns. Based on real-world interoperability testing.

nodes.label: Serve + Attach Pattern (RECOMMENDED)
nodes.rationale: opencode serve (persistent) + opencode run --attach URL. High reliability for automated delegation, CI/CD, batch tasks. Exit 0/1/124. Recommended production pattern.

nodes.label: Gateway Chat Delegation
nodes.rationale: curl POST :8642/v1/chat/completions. High reliability for external client integration. HTTP response.

nodes.label: Hermes Subagent (delegate_task)
nodes.rationale: In-agent task parallelization via delegate_task in Hermes Agent. High reliability. Return value based.

nodes.label: Free Models Require OPENCODE_API_KEY
nodes.rationale: Even 'free' tier opencode/ models require valid OPENCODE_API_KEY for authentication. Config generation creates explicit opencode provider block with apiKey mapping when key is set.

nodes.label: Gateway Restart-Loop Supervisor (delegation context)
nodes.rationale: Gateway wrapped in while true restart loop. Revives after SIGTERM, OOM, crashes. Restart events logged to gateway-restart.log.

nodes.label: opencode run with litellm/ BROKEN
nodes.rationale: OpenCode v1.16.2 hardcoded provider registry for opencode run. Custom providers only load in TUI/serve mode. litellm/ prefix → ProviderModelNotFoundError. Must use opencode/ prefix or serve+attach.

nodes.label: Browser Human-in-the-Loop Stack
nodes.rationale: Interactive Chromium browser shared by Hermes Agent (CDP) and human operator (web VNC). Opt-in, toggled by BROWSER_HUMAN_LOOP_ENABLED. Zero host-side installation.

nodes.label: Xvfb Virtual Display (:99)
nodes.rationale: 1280x720x24 virtual framebuffer. Hardcoded display :99. Required because Chromium needs X server. Runs as hermeswebui.

nodes.label: Chromium CDP Endpoint (:9222)
nodes.rationale: Chrome DevTools Protocol endpoint. Hermes Agent connects via browser.cdp_url in config.yaml. Both agent and human VNC viewer see same tabs/cookies/localStorage.

nodes.label: noVNC + websockify (:6901)
nodes.rationale: Browser-accessible VNC client. websockify bridges WebSocket to VNC. noVNC web assets served from /usr/share/novnc/. Open URL in any browser, type VNC password.

nodes.label: Chromium User Data Persistence
nodes.rationale: User data dir at /home/hermeswebui/.hermes/chrome-debug persists cookies, localStorage, login state across container restarts via bind mount. Critical for maintaining authenticated sessions.

nodes.label: browser.cdp_url Config Injection
nodes.rationale: When enabled, generate_config() injects browser.cdp_url: http://127.0.0.1:9222 into config.yaml. Agent reads at runtime to connect to Chromium DevTools.

nodes.label: Installation A — Active Runtime Detail
nodes.rationale: Located at /app/venv/. Populated by /hermeswebui_init.bash running uv pip install from staged copy at boot. Used by WebUI (in-process AIAgent) and Gateway (CLI binary). Single active runtime.

nodes.label: Installation B — Staged Clone Detail
nodes.rationale: Build-time: /opt/hermes-agent-staging/. Runtime: ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/. Serves three roles: deps source for /hermeswebui_init.bash, skills source for install-skills.sh, readiness marker (pyproject.toml). Never executes agent code directly.

nodes.label: User-Agent Patch Propagation Chain
nodes.rationale: Patch flows: Dockerfile sed → staging → ensure_agent() → ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/ → /hermeswebui_init.bash rsync → /tmp/hermes-agent-build/ → uv pip install → /app/venv/. Multi-step chain delivers patches from build-time to active runtime.

nodes.label: Why Both Installations Exist (Rationale)
nodes.rationale: Base image ships venv but not full agent deps. WebUI init installs from local source tree, not PyPI. Patches must flow through source tree. Bind mount demands runtime copy. Unifying would require deep changes to base image init script (not under our control).

nodes.label: Image Bloat Mitigation Detail
nodes.rationale: Staged clone includes ~200MB unneeded skills/docs/tests/.github. Recommended: add --filter=blob:none or --sparse to git clone, rm -rf non-essential dirs after clone. Preserves pyproject.toml, plugins/, agent source.

nodes.label: docker-compose.override.yml (Cloudflare/Traefik)
nodes.rationale: Auto-loaded by docker compose up. Attaches container to external Cloudflare/Traefik reverse proxy network (portainer-cloudflare-traefik_default). DNS alias vanilla-hermes-opencode for Traefik routing.

nodes.label: docker-compose.ci.yml (CI Port Publishing)
nodes.rationale: NOT auto-loaded. Explicit -f required. Publishes host ports 8787/8642/4096/9222 for E2E test access. Safe to include unconditionally in CI.

nodes.label: Expose-Only Base Compose Rationale
nodes.rationale: Base docker-compose.yml uses expose only (no host port mapping). Production uses reverse proxy network. Different environments need different networking. Override files follow standard Docker Compose merge convention.

nodes.label: Auto-Loading Override Surprise (local dev)
nodes.rationale: Running docker compose up on dev machine without Traefik fails because external network doesn't exist. Use docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up to skip override.

nodes.label: init_wiki() Function (lib/wiki-init.sh)
nodes.rationale: Creates personal knowledge base directory structure at container startup. Idempotent — skips if SCHEMA.md exists. Runs between ensure_agent() and WebUI start. 84 lines.

nodes.label: SCHEMA.md Backbone
nodes.rationale: Defines domain, conventions (lowercase hyphenated filenames, YAML frontmatter, [[wikilinks]]), frontmatter spec, tag taxonomy. Ensures consistent wiki structure across boots.

nodes.label: Wiki Directory Structure
nodes.rationale: raw/articles, raw/papers, raw/transcripts, raw/assets, entities/, concepts/, comparisons/, queries/ + index.md, log.md. Structured for llm-wiki skill ingestion.

nodes.label: Wiki Persistence via Bind Mount
nodes.rationale: Wiki directory lives on Hermes home bind mount. All content persists across container restarts. Optional host sharing via HERMES_WIKI_VOLUME env var.

nodes.label: llm-wiki Skill Integration
nodes.rationale: llm-wiki skill reads WIKI_PATH from environment. Ingests sources into raw/articles/, creates entity/concept pages with [[wikilinks]], maintains index.md, appends to log.md. Sources from skills/research/. Zero network cost — reuses staging clone.

nodes.label: Collect Docker Compose Logs on Failure
nodes.norm_label: collect docker compose logs on failure

nodes.label: Hermes x OpenCode Docker Compose Stack
nodes.norm_label: hermes x opencode docker compose stack

links.source_location: Maps directly to AC in PRD.md
Build hands off to entrypoint at runtime
Both run as hermeswebui via su, sequential startup
Patch enables gateway LLM calls through Cloudflare
HERMES_HOME must point to bind mount
Both expose Hermes Agent via HTTP
Disabled by default because wait -n would tear container
Both are model filter passes in discover_models()
graphify is Source 7 in skill install
cc-safety-net active in all modes including yolo
Both prevent secret exfiltration via different mechanisms
Without patch, agent LLM calls get 403
Runtime graphify re-registration is overlayfs safety net for ARM64
Both are quality-of-life plugins resolved via npm
